purpose: Governance self-healing mechanisms to reduce manual remediation, repair predictable runtime inconsistencies, and improve execution continuity.

rules.condition: execution lock heartbeat expired beyond stale threshold
rules.description: Stale locks prevent new executions and must be auto-released.

rules.condition: markdown reflection exists without corresponding canonical JSON protocol
rules.description: Orphan projections indicate drift between canonical state and generated artifacts.

rules.condition: governance registry missing entry for existing artifact on disk
rules.description: Missing indexes are detected but never auto-created to prevent data loss.

rules.condition: runtime state diverges from expected state derived from event history
rules.description: Runtime divergence indicates corruption or manual tampering.

rules.condition: runtime state file is malformed and cannot be parsed
rules.description: Corrupted state files are destructive to repair; snapshot is mandatory.

rules.condition: expected event sequence shows gaps or out-of-order timestamps
rules.description: Event gaps may indicate dropped events or clock skew.

invariants.expression: every destructive healing action creates a recoverable snapshot first
invariants.description: Snapshots ensure all healing is reversible.

invariants.expression: every healing action emits a governance event and appends to healing log
invariants.description: Complete audit trail for all healing operations.

invariants.expression: destructive healing requires explicit human authorization or --destructive flag
invariants.description: Prevents accidental data loss from automated healing.

triggers.condition: after every execution state transition to blocked or recovering
triggers.description: Immediate healing check when execution enters failure path
